body_txt: Two people are circling each other romantically, or perhaps are already together. Other characters in the story smile fondly at the couple and ship them. And then there's this guy . They do not like the two people together, make their opinion known, and clearly hope that the ship will sink. This may be for a variety of reasons:  And there may be other reasons too. If it progresses far enough, it may result in attempted Relationship Sabotage . Related tropes may include Boyfriend-Blocking Dad , Parental Marriage Veto , and My Sister Is Off-Limits . Considering that this trope shows up at least as far back as Shakespeare , it is Older Than Steam , if not even older. Contrast Shipper on Deck , Shipper with an Agenda , and Romantic Wingman . Compare Moment Killer , when the interfering party only ruins a certain moment and/or may not be intentional; and Ship Sinking , when a ship is sunk by the writers, not characters. For information on actual torpedoes, see Naval Weapons .
